tokens: Once again, we want you to exercise the forward steering procedure here.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: You want to try it first just the way it was and then we'll try the circuit breaker, right?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Pause) The Forward Steering switch should be at Bus Charlie, and the Forward Steering circuit breaker should be finally closed, and then attempt forward steering.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: You know what I bet you did last night, Joe?
You let some of those Marshall guys come up here and fix it, didn't you?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Lot of smiles on that one, Dave.
We might as well use it today.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Boeing has a secret booster somewhere to (send engineers to the Moon to) take care of their Rover!
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Pause) See if I can find a level spot around here.
(Long Pause) Okay Houston, I'm at the Nav site, and I'll reset here, and I'll give you the readings.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: As a matter of fact, to make sure we're balanced, I'll hop off.

(Pause) Okay, roll is one (degree) to the left; pitch is about, oh, one down; and bearing, distance, and range have recycled to zero and the heading on there now is 305; and the Sun shadow device is about, oh, a half to one (degree) to the right.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And when you are ready you can start torquing towards 285, and we'll modify that in a minute.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Want to hop on Jim and see if you can get it...
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I was going to get cameras all adjusted.
Okay, we wanted 12 frames per second.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Jim, while you are climbing on there, we're going to want you to start out towards course 160 for a distance of 1.9 clicks towards Checkpoint Number 1.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Oh, you wait, wait, wait, wait, wait, wait.
(Pause) You've got to sort of bias it to the right.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And, Dave, your torquing angle, exactly, 283, over.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Can you get your seatbelt, Jim?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Pause) Get it up and over the (strap-on thigh) pocket (
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And the motor temps are normal.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Okay, you going to torque to 283 Dave.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: That darn cannon plug again down there.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: You want me to hold it for you.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I have to get off and unhook this thing every time.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: That's bad because every time you bend over like that you put strain on that PLSS antenna.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: It bumps against this low-gain (antenna which is mounted on Dave's inboard handhold).
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Oh, I have to be careful.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, we're off, Joe; we're moving.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (To Joe) Give us that heading again.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: We want you to proceed towards Checkpoint Number 1.
Your general heading is 160 at 1.9 clicks and this may take you down between Salyut and Index Craters.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: I'd like to go down there and see those (two craters), just for drill.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: No one should come to Hadley Rille without seeing Index Crater, and we'll try to keep you posted on when you're coming up on that.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Joe, I'm going to start the (16-mm) camera here.
Will you keep track of it?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Why don't you hold off for awhile, Jim?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Let me get squared away here.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Hey, Joe, the steering is a new task, Joe.
I guess I got pretty used to quiet steering, and this thing really turns!
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: We don't want it to be too easy for you.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: We can always disengage the rear steering.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: No, I'll get used to it, it's just a matter of getting used to.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, on our left, now, we have a very large subdued crater.
It has a crater of about 25 meters (diameter) on its eastern, inner wall, about half way to the bottom.
And on that smaller crater there's some rock exposed.
Looks like some bedrock exposed, in that particular crater.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I'm going to hold up here.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: You want to disengage rear steering, maybe?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And your description is remarkable, remarkably similar to Index.
Perhaps you're looking at Index Crater.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: I guess it's the largest crater that I've seen, Joe, as far as (lost under Joe).
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: We'll reserve judgment on that, but keep describing, please.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Okay, we're heading 155 and at our 1 o'clock position, there's a doublet.
Gee, I think it's the doublet we drove across yesterday.
I'll tell you in a moment when we see our tracks.
Do you want to talk, Dave?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I just want to tell them I turned off the rear steering to see how she works with the front and it's really a lot better.
The double Ackerman's a little too responsive when you have the lack of traction, especially on the slopes.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Why don't we save the double Ackerman for the rougher terrain later on.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: I think that's a good idea.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I mentioned those...It's really a triplet arrangement here that we just passed on our right.
I did not see our tracks.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: So we're definitely east of our track from yesterday.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: ...may very well be coming up on Arbeit Crater.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: There's a fairly fresh one here with angular blocks on the rim.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Look at that one directly ahead, Dave.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: That's Pahoehoe (a Hawaiian term for the smooth, rope-like form of solidified lava).
(Answering Joe) Yeah, the largest ones I would estimate 2 or 3 feet, angular.
There's one on the southeast rim that has a flat top.
In fact, it looks like a rectangular block.
But there are several fragments down there that have the Pahoehoe texture that Dave mentioned yesterday.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And we're doing about 8 to 9 clicks.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay; amp reading is...Let me get my hand down there, Dave (to shadow the gauge).
I want to put my hand down there to get the amps.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, the max looks like about 10 amps.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Pause) Okay, coming up on our right is a very subdued crater again.
No blocks at all on its rim, and it is about 50 meters in diameter.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Let me stop and get the rear steering going.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And I see a very large crater over at 1 o'clock.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, let me just get the steering squared away here, Jim.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Roger, Jim; and that might very well be Earthlight at 1 o'clock.
And Domingo Crater should be on your left, now.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: We'll give you a little more report when we get a little closer to it.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I'm going to hold off too many comments here until Dave gets the steering squared away.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Joe, it feels like the rear steering, when I turn it off, doesn't center.
Feels like my rear wheels are drifting.
So I guess I better turn it back on.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: ...maybe you should turn it back on.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: (To Joe) Yeah, I guess you might think about why they don't center for us.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: That sounds like the Rover moves like Jim's sand crabs move.

tokens: Yeah, that's just exactly what it feels like too.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Well, there's a big thing here in front of us, too.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Yeah, but we could swing...Can you get around to the right?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: No, let's go to the left.
We're not going to stop at Earthlight.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Because that's a big excursion there.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Well, we're going in and out of the craters.

tokens: I get the impression, out to our left, that there is a shallow depression there.

tokens: Gee, over to the left there is a big hole.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Oops, and we're coming up on a sharp one!
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Hey, you got those two ahead of us there?

tokens: See if we can find a reasonable path through here.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, we're heading 140; we're out to 1.0 (km from the LM).
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I think we're going by a very large one here, over at the 9:30...9 o'clock, Jim, huh?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: What do you suppose that is?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Let's see, 1 out...Let's see, Checkpoint 1 is...You said 1.7.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Dave and Jim, that could be, possibly, Index Crater, if you started from where we thought.
The distance is right on that.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Okay, I would say that probably was Index.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, you've got the right bearing.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: There's a nice deep one there that's smooth and rounded, about 30 meters across.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: You know, on one of these trips, we ought to stop at one of these very fresh ones and really sample it.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: On the way back we'll get that one.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I mean these small ones, you know, just filled with...
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: ...rock debris and glass in the middle.
Just do a systematic sampling on it.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Like this one over here at 1 o'clock.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Yeah, I know what you mean.
(Pause) Okay, bearing is now 339 and the range is 1.3.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, Rover, Checkpoint 1 is 160 at 1.9 clicks, and there's no need to stop there.
We can press on towards Checkpoint 2, if you're satisfied.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Okay, we've got the right bearing.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, Jim, and we're enjoying your description.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Why don't you just give them a running comment; I'll keep my eyes on the road.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Lost under Joe)...a crater on our right now about 50 meters in diameter with a lot of gray fragments on its rim.

I can look out now and see the South Cluster and I get the impression of perhaps, some horizontal beds in the first mound in the South Cluster.
I do see a lot of blocks over in that direction; particularly the second mound - the west side of the second mound that appears to be in the Secondary Cluster.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Probably over the area of Crescent.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And, again, we have a very fresh crater on our left with several blocks.

transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Yeah, well, notice all the debris here, that the surface is covered with more debris in this particular area than what we've seen before.

tokens: Okay, I'm going to start the...
transcript.speaker: Irwin

transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, I'm starting the camera, Joe.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: We got the mark, and we'll watch it.

tokens: Reckon we can get between those two there?

transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Boy, there was a very large crater over on our 1 to 2 o'clock position.

tokens: That's the largest one...Oh, I guess it would be equal (to or) maybe larger than...Well, larger than Elbow, certainly.

tokens: You can't see too much of it but it does...

tokens: I don't see that on the map.

tokens: Dave and Jim, we think you might be looking at
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: (Pause) Okay, you would have us east of Earthlight.

tokens: Hey, here's a big deep one here (lost under Joe)

tokens: And Arthur Clarke would be proud of you.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Okay, and on the south...I can just barely see the western slope of Earthlight.

tokens: I don't see any change in the...

tokens: The film apparently is not running through.

tokens: Okay, it's trying to run, Dave.

tokens: Okay, point it to the forward and let's see if it will...I just noticed that the film counter wasn't going.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I hope we don't have another...
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Dave and Jim, the film counter may not have dropped off the hard-stop yet.

We need a range and bearing, if you'll give it to us, please.
And continue on; we'll watch the camera.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Yeah, isn't that what I said?

tokens: Get around this blocky area here.

tokens: Boy, it looks like we're coming down...have to go through a small valley.

tokens: That valley off to the left.

tokens: And that's a north-south trending, isn't it?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I hope we can get through this way.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Oh, the old Rover will make it - with a few little excursions.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: It looks more like a valley, you know, that runs east-west.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Pause) Look at that big hole there, Jim!

tokens: I was wondering whether we could possibly be at Dune.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Boy, that's the biggest one we've seen.

tokens: Yeah, I guess you're right, Joe.

tokens: 'Cause this little one, just to the right of us here, I see it on the map.

tokens: That's a big fella isn't it?

tokens: And you'll be wanting to head more towards the south...towards the numbers 334 and 3.3 clicks.

tokens: Hey, we're in a debris field now, Joe, with fragments on the order of 6 inches to a foot, in general, and maybe, oh, I'd say almost 5, (to) 8 percent coverage.

tokens: And there are some (rocks) that are up to a couple of feet that require some maneuvering.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Let's see, I think I'll go left around this one, Jim, and then swing over to the right.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Lots of...The smaller ones are deeper here.
Man, there's one (meaning a crater) and that's got direction to it (that is, a noticeable asymmetry).

transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Lost under Joe) being about a meter.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: ...just like a secondary impact from the north.

tokens: That's just exactly what it looks like, Joe.

tokens: Yeah, which way do we want to go around?

tokens: Looks like the better way to go from here.

The old Rover's going right up just like it knows what it's doing.

tokens: A little more to the right, Dave.

tokens: Think I'll get up on the rim here where I can take a look.

tokens: Yeah, I'm afraid the rim might be (lost under Joe)

tokens: And turn off the 16-millimeter camera, please.
The film should be run through.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Not a single motion on the little ball on the indicator, Joe.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Yeah, we tried running that through with our fingers last night and I guess it didn't do much good.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: We'll get the next one; don't worry.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Okay, we can definitely look down into Dune Crater.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Man, it sure likes a ray of blocks that run north and south on the southern slope of the crater.

tokens: Yeah, and there's no big rampart like we were thinking we'd see.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, we're heading now 250 to get over on the west side of Dune.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Pause) And, Jim, checkpoint 2 is out at 4.3 clicks...
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: (Pause) And nothing magic about that number as you know.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Yeah, when we get clear of the west side here, Dave, we could just head about 180 (which is south).
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Up again to about 5 to 7 percent.
Partially buried, some of them, and some of them are on the top (that is are sitting on the surface), huh?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And Jim, give us range and bearing, please.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Boy, look up at Hadley Delta...(Stops to listen) Okay, bearing is 348 and range is 3.0.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Look up at that Hadley Delta, Dave.
Don't you get the impression that those craters, (the) secondaries on the side there, are oriented, going right up the slope?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Oh, yeah, you called them right, I think.
I think they just splattered right up the slope.
Because they're the only craters on the side of the mountain.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And they're lined up so nicely!
Good-sized one (meaning a crater) ahead, Dave.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Dave laughs) Okay, we're on about the southwest side, now, of Dune Crater.
As Dave mentioned, we're heading, oh, 155 now.
A very fresh crater at our 1 o'clock position with a lot of angular blocks; very slight raised-rim about 2 feet above the general surface; but a very fresh crater.
It seems like the albedo was lighter around that one than others that we've seen.
In fact, you might be able to see that on your map, Joe.
The lighter albedo in the southwest side of Dune.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And what's the bearing and range to the next checkpoint, Joe?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And you'll want to continue heading south.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Okay, that bearing now is 348 at 3.3.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: ...if we could line up with that chain of secondaries...
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: ...going up the side of Hadley Delta.
But now, look up the slope there at...if you can take...
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: ...your eyes off the road there for a moment.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: As a matter of fact, I'm going to stop right here and take a little break.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: See that large block sitting up about...I'd guess it's a quarter of the way up Hadley Delta.

transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Hey, by the way, we're stopped now, Joe.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (To Joe) I just wanted to take a little break for a minute.
Jim, why don't you pull your camera off and swing it around and get a pan?
Let me hold the maps for you.
(Long Pause) I'll bet you can get, you know, almost 90 or 100 degrees of pan there.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And, Joe, I just put my diverter to Min; and I'd never tried it on the Rover before, but the controls are very easy to reach.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, we got about a 90-degree (pan) there, Dave.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, put your camera back on, and I'll give you your maps back.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: A drink of water and a little bite of
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, we're moving, Joe, by the way.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I think one of those craters there dead ahead, Dave, would probably be Spur.

tokens: Probably the large one at 12 o'clock.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, Dave and Jim, thinking downstream a little bit, we want to drive past Checkpoint 2 (at
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: We're looking in particular for fresh craters, lots of frags, good sampling drill holes into the Front and mare.

tokens: (Pause) And a sweep, and the high water lines and all those good things.

tokens: Incidentally, Joe, thinking back on something we saw yesterday (from Station 2 at St.

transcript.speaker: Scott

Sort of like we're out of the secondaries.

tokens: Dave or Jim, could you give us an estimate of the numbers of rock types you're looking at.
Have you seen two populations so far?
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Oh, it looks like breccia as far as I can tell, Joe, just driving along.

tokens: Yeah, I sort of agree, Joe.

They all look relatively the same.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And press on, troops, with the description.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Okay, we're moving at 10 clicks; we're at 347 on bearing and 3.9 on the range.

tokens: And I'd say the terrain is good for driving, isn't it, Dave?

tokens: Yeah, it's a lot better here.

tokens: (We'll) make better time here along the Front.

tokens: In fact, I bet you we just went by...You know, we just changed terrain type almost distinctly there, Jim.

tokens: You know, we don't have the deep craters anymore.

tokens: There are some fairly good blocks sitting up by themselves there...

tokens: But I guess our primary objective is the crater.

tokens: Dave, do you see Spur as you look up there?

transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: want to go out to this ...

tokens: I guess continue your reconnoiter along the Front.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: I don't know how high we want to go on the Front.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: But we don't want to go too high.
We're...Hey, that must be - maybe to the right there - Spur.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, let's head over to this ridge at 11 o'clock.
I think that's Spur right over there.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: You don't want to hit Spur now?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: No, let's go on down to this rise right in front of us.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, we know where Spur is.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And we're moving along the Front now.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I think we can do a little contour travel here, Jim.
And on the way back pick up that big block up there.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: In other words...I see what you mean: angle uphill.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Boy, it's bright into the Sun, isn't it?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Yeah; you want a map to hold over your eyes?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Talking slightly louder to Houston than he has to Dave) Now, as we drive up-Sun here, I'm looking to the left, and I can see Mount Hadley.
And the linear patterns in it are remarkable.
And the pattern runs from the very top...Well, the whole mountain has the same linear pattern.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And it has the same direction as the dipping beds that I mentioned yesterday that intersected the horizontal beds or high-water marks that Dave just talked about, when we looked at the spur on Mount Hadley.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, see this little crater up on the ridge line here at 1 o'clock?
I think that's where I'll head, Jim.
We'll call that something or another.
And you know, I can see an inflection point (meaning a slope change) here as we go upslope.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (To Joe, distinctly louder) How far east do we want to go?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I think this ought to do it.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Dave and Jim, the first thing we need is just a good sampling stop...
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: A lot of debris (lost under Joe)
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: ...to get a general look around, and we want a crater like Spur or anything similar.
But one that's provided a lot of frags for us and perhaps a lot of rock types to sample.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Well, we haven't seen any the size of Spur just yet.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: There aren't any like that, Joe!
They're all very subtle up here.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I guess we want to continue on towards the east, and keep your eyes open.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Well, we're up on a little ridge here.
And I think it would do well for us to stop here and sample the rocks we can see in this area, and then head over to that boulder, there.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: What do you think about that, Joe?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: However, we're interested now in typical rock types and hopefully, an area that's going to have a lot of fragments around it, not necessarily just one boulder.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Yeah, we have a number of fragments in our local area, none having really been excavated from a particular crater.
There is no crater up here which has excavated a lot of debris.
They're all very subtle and old, but there are rocks on the surface.
So, I think, our best shot here is to hop off and gather up a number of these rocks in our vicinity.
I bet we can get, oh, 10 (to) 12 very easy, and then think about that.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: There's one of those very fresh craters over at 11 o'clock.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: There are several of those around.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Rover power is off and our bearing is...
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Heading) 195, (bearing to the LM) 343, (distance driven) 06.5, (range to the LM) 05.0; (amp-hours remaining) 92, and 100; (battery temperatures) 75 (and) 81; and motor temps are both off-scale low.
transcript.speaker: Irwin
